Great Britain's grid operator Neso paid about £10m on Wednesday to secure enough electricity to meet demand during the heatwave, agreeing to pay about £1,400 a megawatt-hour - nearly 20 times the average electricity market price in June last year - to secure about 1.7 gigawatts of imported electricity from the continent.
